title: Datadog surges nearly 30% after Q1 beat, raises 2026 outlook
text: Datadog Q1 2026 Beat and Guidance Raise. Q1 Results: Datadog reported revenue of 1.01 billion beating consensus estimates of 959.9 million by 4.18 percent. EPS of 0.60 exceeded forecasted 0.51 by 17.65 percent. Stock Reaction: Datadog surged approximately 30 percent on Thursday after reporting blockbuster earnings and lifting its guidance for the year. Market Context: The strong results showed how some software names are starting to prove their case as winners in artificial intelligence. Growth Drivers: Cloud monitoring company's revenue crossed 1 billion for the first time driven by new customers at major AI research labs. AI expansion has become a tailwind for the business since the data-intensive demands of model training and inference push infrastructure complexity higher. Raised Guidance: For the full year Datadog now expects revenue of 4.30 billion to 4.34 billion a meaningful step up from the 4.06 billion to 4.10 billion it had previously projected. Non-GAAP earnings guidance moved to 2.36 to 2.44 per share from an earlier range of 2.08 to 2.16.
